name: Lerna Hydra
description: Lerna was a region in Argolis, near Argos, famous in Greek mythology as the swamp that was home to the Lernaean Hydra, a serpentine water monster with multiple heads. This fearsome creature was one of the offspring of Typhon and Echidna and was raised by Hera, who intended it to be a bane to Heracles. The Hydra possessed poisonous breath and blood, and each time one of its heads was severed, two more would grow back in its place, making it nearly impossible to defeat. The swamp itself was considered a sacred and dangerous place, a gateway to the underworld due to its murky waters and the monstrous presence that dwelled within. It was the location of Heracles' second labor, where he faced the daunting task of slaying the Hydra, a feat he accomplished with the help of Iolaus, who cauterized the necks of the severed heads to prevent them from regrowing. The Hydra's lair was a source of terror and a symbol of the untamed, monstrous forces that heroes like Heracles had to overcome to bring order and civilization to the world. The venom of the Hydra was later used by Heracles to poison his arrows, adding to their lethality.
subtitle: Home of the many-headed serpent.

keyMyths.title: The Second Labor of Heracles
keyMyths.description: Heracles was tasked by King Eurystheus to slay the Lernaean Hydra as his second labor. With the help of Iolaus, he cauterized the Hydra's necks after severing its heads, preventing them from regrowing, and ultimately defeated the monster.

keyMyths.title: Heracles' Poisoned Arrows
keyMyths.description: After slaying the Hydra, Heracles dipped his arrows in its poisonous blood, making them incredibly lethal. These poisoned arrows played a significant role in his later adventures and labors.

sources.author: Apollodorus
sources.description: A comprehensive handbook of Greek mythology.
sources.text: A comprehensive handbook of Greek mythology.

sources.author: Diodorus Siculus
